Attorney General Bonta Charges Clearview AI with Biometric Privacy Violations Under CCPA

January 31, 2026

California Attorney General Rob Bonta today charged Clearview AI, Inc. with violations of the California Consumer Privacy Act for collecting facial recognition biometric data from California residents without consent and selling it to law enforcement and private companies.

Attorney General Bonta Announces $2.1 Million Settlement with Honda Motor Co. for Consumer Warranty Violations

January 9, 2026

Attorney General Rob Bonta today announced a $2.1 million settlement with American Honda Motor Co., Inc. resolving allegations that the automaker denied warranty coverage for known defects on certain vehicle models sold in California, in violation of the Song-Beverly Consumer Warranty Act.

Attorney General Bonta Obtains Judgment Against Acme Charity Fundraisers for Misuse of Donor Funds

December 15, 2025

The California Attorney General's office obtained a $850,000 judgment against Acme Charity Fundraisers LLC, a professional charitable fundraising organization, for diverting donor contributions for personal use and failing to file required nonprofit disclosures under California's Supervision of Trustees and Fundraisers for Charitable Purposes Act.
